name: Ham with Cranberry Cornbread Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 4 cups cornbread crumbs (corn muffin or johnnycake)
5 lb ham, smoked, boned, and rolled
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, melt butter; add onion and celery.
Cook lightly, then stir in mustard, salt, and pepper.
2. Add cranberry sauce and lemon juice. Mix well.
3. Stir in cornbread crumbs until combined.
4. Untie the ham; stuff with cranberry mixture.
5. Re-roll the ham and tie it securely.
6. Place the ham in a roasting pan lightly sprayed with non-stick vegetable cooking spray, fat side up.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Bake for 30 minutes per pound or until a meat thermometer registers 160°F (71°C).
9. In a small saucepan, blend and warm glaze ingredients.
10. After 1-1/2 hours of baking time, remove rind if necessary.
11. Brush the ham surfaces with warm glaze; return to oven.
12. Repeat glazing several times during the remainder of baking time.
Makes 10 servings.
NOTE: Stuffing can be used for pork tenderloin or thick pork chops as well.
